title: malvella leprosa
intro: Malvella leprosa, also known as the hairy-leaved mallow or desert mallow, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Malvella leprosa include treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Malvella leprosa include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically to promote healing and soothe skin conditions.
